contexts.section_title: The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage in London
contexts.text: The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage in London. Ada Lovelace studied the machine and described how punched cards, inspired by the Jacquard loom, could direct symbolic operations.

contexts.section_title: Ada Lovelace translated Luigi Menabrea's article about Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine and expanded it with her own Notes
contexts.text: Ada Lovelace translated Luigi Menabrea's article about Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine and expanded it with her own Notes. Her writing connected the engine to mathematics, music, and reusable sequences of operations.

contexts.section_title: Charles Babbage was a London mathematician associated with the Royal Astronomical Society
contexts.text: Charles Babbage was a London mathematician associated with the Royal Astronomical Society. His Difference Engine targeted numerical tables, while the later Analytical Engine sketched a broader programmable machine.

contexts.section_title: The Jacquard loom used punched cards to control textile weaving patterns
contexts.text: The Jacquard loom used punched cards to control textile weaving patterns. Babbage borrowed the idea of card-directed control when describing the Analytical Engine's store and mill.

contexts.section_title: The Difference Engine was built to automate polynomial calculations for numerical tables
contexts.text: The Difference Engine was built to automate polynomial calculations for numerical tables. Its public demonstrations helped make Charles Babbage's calculating machines known to scientific circles, including readers of Ada Lovelace's later Notes.

contexts.section_title: Luigi Menabrea published an account of the Analytical Engine after hearing Babbage lecture in Turin
contexts.text: Luigi Menabrea published an account of the Analytical Engine after hearing Babbage lecture in Turin. Ada Lovelace's translated Notes added examples that later readers used to discuss early programming.
